title: acinos arvensis
intro: Acinos arvensis, also known as thyme-leaved orphan, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acinos arvensis include reducing symptoms of digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Acinos arvensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
